summary.narrative: Governor Kehoe campaigned on pragmatism and public safety as top priority. Early tenure focused on Safer Missouri initiative with six executive orders. Platform emphasized job creation, workforce development, seniors/veterans support, and education improvement. Limited legislative accomplishments visible in first month; emphasis on executive action and appointments.
summary.grade_rationale: Extremely early in tenure (3 weeks as of data); executive action initiated on signature issue but too soon for substantive outcome measurement. Grade reflects promise scope vs. minimal delivery timeline available.

promises.normalized: Make Missouri easier to be a cop than criminal; hold criminals accountable
Create more jobs and expand workforce development

data_gaps: Specific policy details on job creation targets and workforce programs
Education improvement specifics (funding, curriculum, assessment focus)
Seniors/veterans support program details and budget allocations
Election margin percentage vs. Democratic opponent Crystal Quade
Detailed public safety metrics and criminal justice reform proposals
Legislative agenda and bill priorities for first term
Quantifiable goals and timelines for campaign promises
Status of Safer Missouri initiative outcomes beyond announcement
